source_title: How to Insert Page Number in Word from Specific Page

concepts.name: Section Break
concepts.definition: A formatting tool in Microsoft Word that divides a document into separate sections, allowing for different headers, footers, and page numbering schemes within the same file.

concepts.name: Link to Previous
concepts.definition: A setting in the Header/Footer menu that determines whether the current section's header/footer inherits the content and formatting of the preceding section.

procedures.name: Insert Page Numbers Starting from a Specific Page

steps.action: Place cursor at the bottom of the page immediately preceding the page where numbering should start.
steps.explanation: To define the boundary between the unnumbered/differently numbered section and the new section.
steps.gotcha: Ensure the cursor is at the very end of the text to avoid creating unnecessary empty pages.

steps.explanation: This creates a Section Break, separating the document into Section 1 and Section 2.

steps.action: Double-click the header or footer area on the target page.
steps.explanation: To activate the Header & Footer Tools menu.

steps.action: Click 'Link to Previous' to deselect/disable it.
steps.explanation: This breaks the connection between the current section and the previous one, allowing independent page numbering.
steps.gotcha: If this is not disabled, page numbers will still appear on the previous pages.

steps.explanation: To specify the starting number for the new section.

steps.action: Select 'Start at' and enter '1' (or the desired starting number), then click OK.
steps.explanation: Forces the sequence to begin at a specific digit rather than continuing from the previous section.

steps.action: Go to 'Insert' -> 'Page Number' and select the desired position (Top/Bottom).
steps.explanation: To physically place the numbers on the page.

gotchas.description: Page numbers appearing on all pages despite section breaks.
gotchas.context: Happens when the 'Link to Previous' option remains enabled in the Header/Footer settings.
gotchas.solution: Navigate to the Header/Footer of the second section and click 'Link to Previous' to turn it off before inserting page numbers.

gotchas.description: Page numbering starting at the wrong digit (e.g., starting at 3 instead of 1).
gotchas.context: Happens when 'Continue from previous section' is selected in the Page Number Format menu.
gotchas.solution: Change the setting to 'Start at:' and manually enter '1'.
